claimText: Sunbird is a nuclear fusion-based rocket being developed by Pulsar Fusion (UK) and Princeton Satellite Systems (USA).
evidenceExcerpt: A nuclear fusion-based rocket being developed by Pulsar Fusion (UK) and Princeton Satellite Systems (USA).
examUse: Statement-based question on emerging space technologies and their developers.
distractorHooks: Nuclear fission instead of nuclear fusion
Developed by NASA or ISRO instead of Pulsar Fusion and Princeton Satellite Systems

claimText: The Dual Direct Fusion Drive (DDFD) technology used in the Sunbird rocket generates both thrust and electricity.
evidenceExcerpt: Powered by Dual Direct Fusion Drive (DDFD) technology... DDFD generates both thrust and electricity...
examUse: Statement-based question on the capabilities and dual-use nature of DDFD technology in space missions.
distractorHooks: Generates only thrust, requiring separate solar panels or RTGs for electricity
Uses chemical propulsion instead of direct fusion drive

claimText: The Sunbird rocket's Dual Direct Fusion Drive is powered by a combination of helium-3 and deuterium.
evidenceExcerpt: Using helium-3 and deuterium, DDFD generates both thrust and electricity...
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question on the specific fuels used in advanced nuclear fusion space propulsion.
distractorHooks: Uses liquid hydrogen and liquid oxygen
